Question: For the following limit, define $$a , f ( x ) ,$$ and $$L .$$ $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 5 \right ) }{lim} ( 2 x^{2} - 4 ) = 46$$

Using the graph of the function $$y = f ( x )$$ shown in Figure 8 , estimate the following limits. $$\underset{x \rightarrow 0^{-}}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right)$$ $$\underset{x \rightarrow 0^{+}}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right)$$ $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 0 \right ) }{ \left ( l i m \right ) } f ( x )$$ $$\underset{x \rightarrow 2^{-}}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right)$$ $$\underset{x \rightarrow 2^{+}}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right)$$ $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 2 \right ) }{ \left ( l i m \right ) } f ( x )$$ $$\underset{x \rightarrow 4^{-}}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right)$$ $$\underset{x \rightarrow 4^{+}}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right)$$ $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 4 \right ) }{ \left ( l i m \right ) } f ( x )$$ Figure 8
Answer: a. 0; b. 2; c. does not exist; d. $$- 2 ;$$ e. 0; f. does not exist; g. 4; h. 4; i. 4

Question: Numerically estimate the limit of the following function by making a table: $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 0 \right ) }{lim} ( \frac{20 \sin{} ( x )}{4 x} )$$
Numerically estimate the following limit: $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 0 \right ) }{lim} ( \sin{( \frac{2}{x} )} ) .$$
